data.title: Worker strike slows production at B2Gold's flagship Mali mine - Reuters
data.description:
A workers strike at B2Gold's (NYSE:BTG) Fekola mine in Mali has reduced mining operations to just 25% of capacity, Reuters reported Wednesday.

As many as 2,000 workers at the company's flagship mine launched the seven-day strike on November 29 over the dismissal of some of their colleagues, according to the report, which cited two employees.

B2Gold (BTG) said operations at Fekola were continuing at a reduced capacity, although "well in excess" of the 25% reported by the sources.

The company said earlier this week that the strike action was triggered by steps it took against a few employees who had engaged in alleged illegal activities.

The Fekola plant was operating normally and production was expected to come in near the lower end of a previous forecast range of 420K-450K oz of gold this year, the company said.

data.title: Toast down after presentation at a UBS conference
data.description:
• Toast (NYSE:TOST) fell ~10% on Wednesday afternoon after the digital technology platform's presentation at a UBS conference.
  • Stephens analyst Charles Nabhan noted that TOST sees a 2025 margin expansion of ~100 basis points to 200 basis points, compared to the Wall Street expectations of above 380 basis points.

data.title: EU begins questioning Nvidia customers as part of Run.ai scrutiny: report
data.description:
• The European Commission has started to question some of Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) customers if they have received discounts to buy its GPU software products along with its GPUs, Reuters reported.
  • Shares of the tech giant were up more than 3.5% in afternoon trading.
  • The survey to Nvidia's customers focused on some business practices that could further add to the Jensen Huang-led company's market share in GPUs geared towards artificial intelligence, the news outlet added. The inquiry is part of the European Commission's potential probe into Nvidia's proposed $700M acquisition of Run:ai.
  • Nvidia did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • In October, the European Commission said that although the deal does not meet the notification thresholds set forth by the EU Merger Regulation, Italy expressed concern over the deal, via the Italian Competition Act.
  • Nvidia announced its deal to acquire Run:ai in April in an effort to make AI chips more efficient by enabling multiple workloads to run in parallel, reducing the number of GPUs needed to complete tasks.

data.title: AstraZeneca names new head of Chinese operations
data.description:
• AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) has appointed Iskra Reic as executive vice president for its international operations, succeeding Leon Wang who has been on extended leave due to an ongoing investigation by Chinese authorities.
  • Reic will be in charge of the company’s strategy and sustainable growth in Asia, Eurasia, the Middle East, Africa and Latin America, including Australia, New Zealand and China. She joined AstraZeneca (AZN) in 2001, mostly recently overseeing the launch of its Vaccines and Immune Therapies unit, according to a statement.
  • AstraZeneca’s (AZN) operations have been under investigation in China over suspected insurance fraud and the alleged importation of drugs into mainland China. Wang was detained by Chinese authorities in late October.

data.title: ETFs hit record inflows in November and are on pace to surpass $1T by the end of 2024
data.description:
November proved to be a noteworthy month for the U.S. equity space, as it delivered a 4% return, driving record-breaking inflows into exchange traded funds.

A notable result of the market performance was a record inflow into exchange traded funds, which saw $164B in new investments in November—22% more than the previous monthly record set in December 2023. With year-to-date ETF inflows now at $993B, the industry is on track to surpass $1.1T in total inflows by the end of 2024.

U.S. equity ETFs were the biggest beneficiaries on the month, attracting a record $122B, helping drive the three-month differential between U.S. and non-U.S. equity ETFs to a historic $188B. Sector-specific ETFs also saw strong inflows, particularly those targeting cyclical stocks, which added $11B.

Bond ETFs also had a significant showing, with $28B moving into these products in November. From the influx, $12B went into risk-on credit sectors. However, government bond ETFs experienced outflows, as investors favored riskier assets in the current market environment.

The strong ETF inflows reflect a broader trend of investors seeking exposure to sectors and assets benefiting from the ongoing market rally, bolstering the optimism around a balanced portfolio of stocks and bonds.

Highlighted below are Wall Street’s top 20 largest ETFs from an assets under management vantage point:

data.title: AT&T, JPMorgan have reportedly pulled out of Twitch ads after allegations of antisemitism
data.description:
AT&T (NYSE:T), JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM), and Dunkin’ Brands have halted their advertising on Twitch after a controversial group of live streamers alleged that the company is promoting antisemitic content, according to a Bloomberg report from Wednesday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Chevron (NYSE:CVX) is also considering ending future support for the TwitchCon convention after its name appeared in the background of a controversial panel discussion at the annual event, according to Bloomberg.

The report said the pressure is coming from online forums and chat rooms associated with Steven “Destiny” Bonnell, who was once a popular political commentator on Twitch but was banned in 2022 for “hateful conduct." Bonnell has attacked rival commentator Hasan Piker, a popular Twitch streamer, including for his criticism of Israel. Piker describes himself as “an advocate for Palestinian emancipation, but also a fervent combatter of antisemitism.”

"Despite what a handful of online personalities claim, we do not tolerate antisemitism on Twitch,” a Twitch spokesperson said. The spokesperson said that the company's guidelines apply to all streamers “consistently and objectively.”

“Supporting advertisers that engage our community to promote their brands and products is critical to Twitch,” the Twitch spokesperson reportedly said.

data.title: Williams CEO pledges 'very large lawsuit' against Energy Transfer in pipeline fight
data.description:
Williams (NYSE:WMB) plans to bring a "very large lawsuit" against Energy Transfer (ET) after a legal dispute between the companies delayed construction of the Louisiana Energy Gateway project, CEO Alan Armstrong told Argus in an interview on Tuesday.

Energy Transfer (ET) is the only company in "pipeline history" to have defied industry norms over pipeline crossings in a bid to block competitors' projects, Armstrong told Argus, and he hopes the lawsuit would "stop the industry from spiraling into that kind of behavior."

Energy Transfer (ET) tried to block Williams (WMB) and other pipeline companies from building new gas pipelines across its own Tiger pipeline in Louisiana's Haynesville shale, arguing that projects by Williams and others proposed an excessive number of crossings under and over its own pipelines; opponents argued Energy Transfer was just attempting to control market share.

Beyond trying to block Williams (WMB) from crossing the Tiger pipeline, Energy Transfer (ET) also sought a federal review of Williams' proposed 1.8B cf/day Louisiana Energy Gateway pipeline as an interstate transmission line, rather than a gathering line.

Energy Transfer (ET) lost cases to Williams (WMB) in lawsuits spanning parishes across Louisiana, but the litigation pushed back Louisiana Energy Gateway's in-service date from late 2024 to H2 2025.

data.title: IGM Financial reports a 16.4% Y/Y increase in November AUM and advisement
data.description:
• Canada-based asset manager IGM Financial (TSX:IGM:CA) reported assets under management and advisement of $273.4B at November 2024-end, compared to $234.9B at the end of November 30, 2023, and $264.8B in the prior month-end.
  • The company reported net inflows of $50M during November 2024, compared to outflows of $396M in November 2023.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Thunderbird Entertainment Group announces commencement of normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Thunderbird Entertainment Group (OTCQX:THBRF) has received approval from the TSX Venture Exchange of its notice of intention to make a normal course issuer bid.
  • The company may purchase for cancellation up to 3.19M common shares representing ~10% of its public float over a twelve-month period commencing on December 9, 2024.
  • The normal course issuer bid will expire no later than December 8, 2025.
  • The company has an existing normal course issuer bid in effect, which began on December 7, 2023 and is set to expire on December 6, 2024. THBRF has repurchased for cancellation 591,400 common shares for total consideration of $1.23M, representing an average price of $2.08 per common share.
  • The company intends to fund the purchases out of available cash.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Absci a new buy at Jones on AI platform to design antibodies
data.description:
• Jones has initiated Absci (NASDAQ:ABSI) at buy citing the drug creation company's AI platform that is used to create biologic drugs.
  • The firm has a $9 price target (~201% upside based on Dec. 3 close).
  • Analyst Debanjana Chatterjee said that Absci differentiates itself from rivals "with its large scale proprietary training data, zero-shot generative AI model for de novo antibody design, and integrated high throughput in house wet lab screening and validation capacity."
  • Adding to her bullishness is that the company Absci has already signed several deals with other drug companies, and has demonstrated design milestones, an achievement that has validated its technology. "Advancement of partnered products will not only generate meaningful cash flows through milestone payments and royalties, but also likely attract further partnerships."
  • Chatterjee highlighted Absci's lead asset, ABS-101 for inflammatory bowel disease, as the best-in-class anti-TL1A. She added that TL1A is a clinically validated target and ABS-101 could potentially bring in $4B in peak revenue. First-in-human data is expected in H2 2025.
